metadata.name: Apply Database Migration
metadata.purpose: Generic database migration runbook with comprehensive safety checks, automated backup, dry-run capability, and rollback procedures for applying any database schema migration
metadata.author: Claude (Enterprise Integration)

executiveSummary.description: Enterprise-grade database migration runbook providing a systematic, safe approach to applying database schema changes. Features comprehensive pre-flight checks, automated backup creation, SQL validation, dry-run mode for testing, post-migration verification, and complete rollback capability. Designed to minimize risk and ensure data integrity during schema modifications.
keyChanges: Comprehensive pre-flight validation of database connectivity
Automated full database backup before changes
Complete rollback procedure with backup restore
Detailed success criteria and validation queries
prerequisites: Target database exists (ectropy_dev, ectropy_staging, or ectropy_production)
Docker CLI available for database operations
Sufficient disk space for backup creation (recommend 500MB+ free)
Migration SQL file exists in libs/database/migrations/
Dependent services can be restarted if needed

impact.downtime: None for additive migrations, minimal (< 30s) for destructive changes
impact.dataLoss: None with backup (backup created automatically before changes)

preFlightChecks.name: Validate migration file exists
preFlightChecks.description: Ensure specified migration SQL file exists in migrations directory
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: File must exist at specified path before proceeding

onFailure.message: Migration file not found at specified path. Verify file path and try again.

preFlightChecks.name: Validate PostgreSQL container is running
preFlightChecks.description: Verify database container exists and is in running state
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: Container must be running to accept connections

onFailure.message: PostgreSQL container not running. Start with: docker-compose up -d postgres

preFlightChecks.name: Validate PostgreSQL connectivity
preFlightChecks.description: Ensure database container is accepting connections
preFlightChecks.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -c "SELECT version()"
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: Exit code 0 indicates successful database connection

onFailure.message: Cannot connect to PostgreSQL. Database may be starting. Wait 10s and retry.

preFlightChecks.name: Verify target database exists
preFlightChecks.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -lqt | cut -d \| -f 1 | grep -qw ectropy_dev
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: Target database must exist before applying migration

onFailure.message: Target database does not exist. Create it first or check database name.

preFlightChecks.name: Check if migration already applied
preFlightChecks.description: Detect if migration tables already exist to prevent duplicate application
preFlightChecks.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'speckle_streams');"
preFlightChecks.expectedResult: f (false) if migration not yet applied
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: If migration tables exist, warn user before proceeding

onFailure.message: Migration may have been applied already. Verify before continuing.

preFlightChecks.name: Verify sufficient disk space for backup
preFlightChecks.description: Ensure adequate disk space for database backup creation
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: Minimum 500MB free space recommended for backup

onFailure.message: Low disk space. Backup may fail. Free up space or use -SkipBackup (not recommended).

preFlightChecks.name: Basic SQL syntax validation
preFlightChecks.description: Check SQL file is readable and contains valid structure
preFlightChecks.expectedResult: At least one CREATE TABLE statement found
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: SQL should contain expected DDL statements

onFailure.message: No CREATE TABLE statements found. Verify migration SQL content.

preFlightChecks.name: Check for destructive operations
preFlightChecks.description: Warn if migration contains DROP TABLE or TRUNCATE statements
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: Flag potentially destructive operations for operator review

onFailure.message: CAUTION: Migration contains potentially destructive operations (DROP/TRUNCATE)

phases.name: Pre-Migration Validation
phases.description: Comprehensive safety checks before migration execution

steps.name: Verify migration file exists
steps.description: Check that the specified migration SQL file is present and accessible
steps.command: if (-not (Test-Path $migrationFile)) { Write-Host '[FAILED] Migration file not found: $migrationFile' -ForegroundColor Red; exit 1 }
steps.purpose: Ensure migration file is available before proceeding with any operations

validation.criteria: Migration file must exist at specified path

onFailure.message: Migration file not found. Verify path and try again.

steps.name: Display migration summary
steps.description: Show operator which migration will be applied and to which database
steps.command: Write-Host 'Migration file: $migrationFile' -ForegroundColor Cyan; Write-Host 'Target database: ectropy_dev' -ForegroundColor Cyan
steps.purpose: Provide clear visibility into migration scope

onFailure.message: Could not display summary, but proceeding

steps.name: Check PostgreSQL connectivity
steps.description: Verify database container is running and accepting connections
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -c "SELECT version()" > $null 2>&1; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Write-Host '[FAILED] Cannot connect to PostgreSQL' -ForegroundColor Red; exit 1 }
steps.purpose: Ensure database is available before attempting migration

onFailure.message: Cannot connect to PostgreSQL. Is the database running? Check: docker ps

steps.name: Check migration status
steps.description: Verify if migration has already been applied to prevent duplicate execution
steps.command: $tablesCheck = docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'speckle_streams');" 2>&1; if ($tablesCheck -match 't') { Write-Host '[WARN] Tables may already exist' -ForegroundColor Yellow }
steps.purpose: Prevent accidental reapplication of migrations

validation.criteria: Should return 'f' if migration not applied

onFailure.message: Migration may have been applied. Continue anyway? (yes/no)

phases.name: Database Backup Creation
phases.description: Create full database backup before applying any changes

steps.name: Create backup directory
steps.description: Ensure backup directory structure exists
steps.command: if (-not (Test-Path $backupDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $backupDir -Force | Out-Null }
steps.purpose: Prepare directory for backup file storage

onFailure.message: Failed to create backup directory

steps.name: Execute pg_dump backup
steps.description: Create timestamped full database backup using PostgreSQL pg_dump utility
steps.command: $backupFile = "$backupDir/ectropy_dev_pre_migration_$timestamp.sql"; docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 pg_dump -U postgres ectropy_dev > $backupFile 2>$null; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Write-Host '[FAILED] Database backup failed' -ForegroundColor Red; exit 1 }
steps.purpose: Enable complete rollback capability if migration fails

validation.criteria: Backup file must be created successfully

onFailure.message: Database backup failed. Migration aborted for safety. Check disk space and permissions.

notes: Backup includes complete database schema and all data
Typical backup size: 50-500 KB for development, larger for production
Backup file naming pattern: ectropy_dev_pre_migration_YYYYMMDD_HHmmss.sql
pg_dump creates a SQL script that can be used to restore database

steps.name: Verify backup file created
steps.description: Confirm backup file exists and report size
steps.command: $backupSize = (Get-Item $backupFile).Length / 1KB; Write-Host "[OK] Backup created ($([math]::Round($backupSize, 2)) KB)" -ForegroundColor Green
steps.purpose: Confirm backup was successfully written to disk with non-zero size

validation.criteria: Backup file size must be > 0 bytes

onFailure.message: Backup file verification failed. File may be empty or corrupted.

phases.name: Migration SQL Validation
phases.description: Validate migration SQL structure, syntax, and safety

steps.name: Load and parse SQL content
steps.description: Read migration SQL file into memory for analysis
steps.command: $sqlContent = Get-Content $migrationFile -Raw
steps.purpose: Prepare SQL content for validation checks

onFailure.message: Cannot read migration SQL file. Check file permissions.

steps.name: Validate expected table creation
steps.description: Check SQL contains expected CREATE TABLE statements
steps.command: if ($sqlContent -notmatch 'CREATE TABLE.*speckle_streams') { Write-Host '[WARN] Expected table creation not found' -ForegroundColor Yellow; $validationPassed = $false }
steps.purpose: Ensure migration contains expected schema changes

validation.criteria: SQL must contain expected table definitions

onFailure.message: Expected table creation statements not found in migration

steps.name: Check for dangerous DROP statements
steps.description: Warn if migration contains potentially destructive DROP TABLE operations
steps.command: if ($sqlContent -match 'DROP TABLE') { Write-Host '[WARN] Migration contains DROP TABLE statements' -ForegroundColor Yellow }
steps.purpose: Alert operator to potentially destructive operations requiring extra caution

validation.criteria: Ideally should not contain DROP TABLE for additive migrations

onFailure.message: Migration contains DROP TABLE - proceed with caution and verify backup

steps.name: Count migration statements
steps.description: Analyze and count different types of DDL statements in migration
steps.command: $createTableCount = ([regex]::Matches($sqlContent, 'CREATE TABLE', [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::IgnoreCase)).Count; $createIndexCount = ([regex]::Matches($sqlContent, 'CREATE INDEX', [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::IgnoreCase)).Count; $alterTableCount = ([regex]::Matches($sqlContent, 'ALTER TABLE', [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::IgnoreCase)).Count; $createViewCount = ([regex]::Matches($sqlContent, 'CREATE VIEW', [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::IgnoreCase)).Count
steps.purpose: Provide migration impact summary for operator review

validation.criteria: Statement counts should be reasonable (< 100 of each type)

onFailure.message: Statement counting failed, but migration can continue

steps.name: Display dry-run summary (if applicable)
steps.description: Show operator what would be executed in dry-run mode
steps.command: if ($DryRun) { Write-Host 'Would execute:' -ForegroundColor Cyan; Write-Host "- $createTableCount table creation(s)" -ForegroundColor White; Write-Host "- $createIndexCount index creation(s)" -ForegroundColor White; Write-Host "- $alterTableCount table alteration(s)" -ForegroundColor White; Write-Host "- $createViewCount view creation(s)" -ForegroundColor White }
steps.purpose: Inform operator of migration scope without making changes

onFailure.message: Could not display dry-run summary

phases.name: Migration Execution
phases.description: Apply migration SQL to database (skipped in dry-run mode)

steps.name: Record migration start time
steps.description: Capture start timestamp for duration calculation
steps.command: $migrationStart = Get-Date; Write-Host 'Applying Migration...' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Write-Host 'This may take 30-60 seconds...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
steps.purpose: Track migration execution time for performance metrics

onFailure.message: Could not record start time

steps.name: Execute migration SQL
steps.description: Apply migration to target database using psql
steps.command: docker exec -i ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev < $migrationFile 2>&1 | Out-Null; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Write-Host '[FAILED] Migration failed' -ForegroundColor Red; Write-Host 'Check logs for errors' -ForegroundColor Yellow; if (-not $SkipBackup) { Write-Host "[INFO] Backup available at: $backupFile" -ForegroundColor Cyan; Write-Host "To restore: docker exec -i ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres ectropy_dev < $backupFile" -ForegroundColor Cyan } exit 1 }
steps.purpose: Create or modify database schema according to migration SQL

validation.criteria: Migration must complete without PostgreSQL errors

onFailure.message: Migration failed. Review PostgreSQL logs for error details. Backup available for rollback.

notes: Migration is executed within a transaction (if SQL includes BEGIN/COMMIT)
PostgreSQL errors will cause automatic rollback of transaction
Output is suppressed but errors are captured in exit code
Typical execution time: 30-120 seconds depending on complexity

steps.name: Calculate and display migration duration
steps.description: Calculate total migration execution time and display to operator
steps.command: $duration = ((Get-Date) - $migrationStart).TotalSeconds; Write-Host "[OK] Migration applied successfully ($([math]::Round($duration, 2))s)" -ForegroundColor Green
steps.purpose: Provide performance metrics and confirm successful completion

onFailure.message: Duration calculation failed, but migration succeeded

steps.name: Verify tables created
steps.description: Check that expected tables exist in database schema
steps.command: $tables = @('speckle_streams', 'speckle_sync_logs'); foreach ($table in $tables) { $exists = docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = '$table');" 2>&1; if ($exists -match 't') { Write-Host "[OK] Table '$table' exists" -ForegroundColor Green } else { Write-Host "[FAIL] Table '$table' not found" -ForegroundColor Red; $validationPassed = $false } }
steps.purpose: Confirm primary tables were created successfully

onFailure.message: Required tables not found - migration incomplete, consider rollback

steps.name: Verify views created
steps.description: Check that expected database views exist
steps.command: $views = @('v_active_speckle_syncs', 'v_project_speckle_streams'); foreach ($view in $views) { $exists = docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = '$view');" 2>&1; if ($exists -match 't') { Write-Host "[OK] View '$view' exists" -ForegroundColor Green } else { Write-Host "[WARN] View '$view' not found" -ForegroundColor Yellow } }
steps.purpose: Confirm support views were created (non-critical)

validation.criteria: Views should exist for monitoring and reporting

onFailure.message: Some views not found - may impact monitoring but not core functionality

steps.name: Verify columns added to existing tables
steps.description: Check that new columns were added to existing tables
steps.command: $columnCheck = docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'construction_elements' AND column_name = 'speckle_object_id');" 2>&1; if ($columnCheck -match 't') { Write-Host '[OK] Column speckle_object_id added to construction_elements' -ForegroundColor Green } else { Write-Host '[WARN] Column speckle_object_id not found' -ForegroundColor Yellow }
steps.purpose: Confirm ALTER TABLE operations succeeded

validation.criteria: New columns should be present in modified tables

onFailure.message: Expected columns not found in existing tables

steps.name: Test basic query execution
steps.description: Execute test query on new tables to verify accessibility
steps.command: $testQuery = 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM speckle_streams;'; docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "$testQuery" > $null 2>&1; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { Write-Host '[OK] Can query speckle_streams table' -ForegroundColor Green } else { Write-Host '[FAIL] Cannot query speckle_streams table' -ForegroundColor Red; $validationPassed = $false }
steps.purpose: Verify tables are queryable and accessible (permissions correct)

onFailure.message: Cannot query new tables - possible permission or constraint issue

steps.name: Verify indexes created
steps.description: Check that performance indexes were created successfully
steps.command: $indexCount = docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename IN ('speckle_streams', 'speckle_sync_logs');" 2>&1; Write-Host "[INFO] Created $indexCount indexes" -ForegroundColor Cyan
steps.purpose: Confirm performance optimization indexes are in place

validation.criteria: Expected number of indexes should be created

onFailure.message: Index count unexpected - may impact query performance

phases.name: Post-Migration Guidance
phases.description: Provide operator with next steps and deployment guidance

steps.name: Display final migration summary
steps.description: Show comprehensive summary of migration results
steps.command: Write-Host '========================================' -ForegroundColor Cyan; Write-Host 'MIGRATION SUMMARY' -ForegroundColor Cyan; Write-Host '========================================' -ForegroundColor Cyan; if ($DryRun) { Write-Host '[DRY RUN] No changes were made' -ForegroundColor Cyan } elseif ($validationPassed) { Write-Host '[SUCCESS] Migration applied and verified' -ForegroundColor Green } else { Write-Host '[PARTIAL] Migration applied but verification failed' -ForegroundColor Yellow }
steps.purpose: Provide clear migration outcome to operator

onFailure.message: Could not display summary

steps.name: Display backup location
steps.description: Show operator where backup file is stored for rollback if needed
steps.command: if (-not $SkipBackup -and -not $DryRun) { Write-Host 'Backup Location:' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Write-Host "  $backupFile" -ForegroundColor White }
steps.purpose: Inform operator of rollback capability

onFailure.message: Could not display backup location

steps.name: Display next steps
steps.description: Show recommended post-migration actions
steps.command: Write-Host 'Next Steps:' -ForegroundColor Cyan; Write-Host '  1. Restart API Gateway to pick up schema changes' -ForegroundColor White; Write-Host '     docker-compose restart api-gateway' -ForegroundColor White; Write-Host '  2. Create Speckle admin user at http://localhost:8080' -ForegroundColor White; Write-Host '  3. Run smoke tests: .\scripts\deployment\smoke-test.ps1' -ForegroundColor White
steps.purpose: Guide operator through post-migration deployment steps

onFailure.message: Could not display next steps

dataIntegrity.validation: Migration is additive - no data deletion or modification of existing records
dataIntegrity.expected: All existing data intact after migration

dataIntegrity.validation: All foreign keys reference existing tables
All performance indexes created without errors

performanceThresholds.measurement: Total time from start to completion of SQL execution

triggerConditions: Migration SQL execution fails with PostgreSQL errors
Required tables or columns not created after migration
Foreign key or check constraint violations detected
Manual operator decision due to unexpected behavior
Critical application errors after migration deployment

steps.name: Locate backup file
steps.command: Get-ChildItem -Path backups/migrations/ -Filter 'ectropy_dev_pre_migration_*' | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
steps.purpose: Find the most recent pre-migration backup with timestamp
notes: Backup files are named: ectropy_dev_pre_migration_YYYYMMDD_HHmmss.sql
Use most recent backup if multiple exist from same migration attempt
Verify backup file size is reasonable (> 10 KB) before restoring

steps.name: Stop dependent services
steps.command: docker-compose stop api-gateway web-dashboard mcp-server
steps.purpose: Prevent active database connections during restore to avoid conflicts
notes: This ensures no services are actively using the database during restore
Services will be restarted after rollback completion
Typically takes 10-20 seconds for graceful shutdown

steps.name: Drop new tables (if created)
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "DROP TABLE IF EXISTS speckle_sync_logs CASCADE; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS speckle_streams CASCADE;"
steps.purpose: Remove newly created tables before full restore to prevent conflicts
notes: CASCADE ensures dependent objects (views, triggers) are also removed
This step prevents primary key or unique constraint conflicts during restore
Only drops tables that were created by migration

steps.name: Restore from backup
steps.command: docker exec -i ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev < $backupFile
steps.purpose: Restore complete database to pre-migration state
notes: Full database restore including all schema and data
Restore duration: 1-3 minutes depending on database size
All changes made by migration will be reverted
Any data inserted after migration will be lost

steps.name: Verify tables removed
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name IN ('speckle_streams', 'speckle_sync_logs');"
steps.purpose: Confirm migration tables were successfully removed
notes: Expected result: count = 0
If count > 0, tables still exist - manual cleanup may be needed
This verifies rollback was successful

steps.name: Verify columns removed from modified tables
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'construction_elements' AND column_name = 'speckle_object_id');"
steps.purpose: Confirm added columns were removed from existing tables
notes: Expected result: f (false)
If t (true), column still exists - may need manual ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN
This ensures complete rollback of schema changes

steps.name: Restart application services
steps.command: docker-compose start api-gateway web-dashboard mcp-server
steps.purpose: Restore application services to operational state after rollback
notes: Services should start normally with pre-migration schema
Check service logs for any startup errors
Typically takes 30-60 seconds for all services to become healthy

steps.name: Verify database connectivity and functionality
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "SELECT version(); SELECT COUNT(*) FROM projects;"
steps.purpose: Confirm database is operational and accessible after rollback
notes: Should return PostgreSQL version and project count
Confirms database is accessible and functional
If queries fail, database may need additional recovery steps

checks.name: Migration tables removed
checks.command: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name LIKE 'speckle_%'

checks.name: Migration views removed
checks.command: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_name LIKE 'v_%speckle%'

checks.name: Added columns removed from existing tables
checks.command: SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'construction_elements' AND column_name IN ('speckle_object_id', 'element_type', 'geometry_data')

checks.name: Database functional with existing data
checks.command: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM projects; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM construction_elements;
checks.expectedResult: Queries execute successfully, counts match pre-migration values

verification.successCriteria: All migration-specific database objects removed, pre-migration schema restored, existing data intact, services operational

postRollbackActions: Investigate root cause of migration failure by reviewing PostgreSQL logs
Review migration SQL for syntax errors, constraint violations, or incompatibilities
Check that all prerequisite tables and schemas exist (from previous migrations)
Verify PostgreSQL version compatibility with migration SQL syntax
Re-run migration with --DryRun flag to validate before reapplication
Consider breaking migration into smaller, incremental steps if complexity is high
Document failure reason and resolution for future reference
Update migration SQL to fix identified issues before re-attempting

monitoring.duration: Critical: First 24 hours after migration | Ongoing: Continuous monitoring

keyMetrics.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM speckle_streams; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM speckle_sync_logs;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.table_constraints WHERE constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY' AND table_name IN ('speckle_streams', 'speckle_sync_logs')
Review application logs for database-related errors
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM speckle_streams LIMIT 10

alertThresholds.condition: Database queries fail with schema errors
alertThresholds.action: Check schema integrity, verify migration completed successfully
alertThresholds.escalation: Immediate rollback if critical tables missing or corrupted

alertThresholds.action: Review migration for constraint definition errors
alertThresholds.escalation: Consider rollback if data integrity compromised

alertThresholds.condition: Application cannot connect to new tables
alertThresholds.action: Check RLS policies and permissions on new tables
alertThresholds.escalation: Grant appropriate permissions or rollback if permissions cannot be resolved

alertThresholds.action: Review indexes created, check query execution plans
alertThresholds.escalation: Add missing indexes or optimize queries

dashboards: Database health: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -c '\l+'
Table statistics: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_user_tables WHERE schemaname = 'public'
Index usage: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_user_indexes WHERE schemaname = 'public'
Connection count: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = 'ectropy_dev'
Recent errors: Review PostgreSQL logs for ERROR or FATAL messages

successCriteria.validation: Exit code 0 from psql command execution
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name IN ('<expected_tables>')
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_name IN ('<expected_views>')

successCriteria.criterion: New columns added to existing tables
successCriteria.validation: SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = '<table>' AND column_name = '<column>')
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename IN ('<new_tables>') >= <expected_count>

successCriteria.criterion: Foreign key constraints valid and enforced
successCriteria.validation: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.table_constraints WHERE constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY'

successCriteria.criterion: Row Level Security (RLS) enabled if required
successCriteria.validation: SELECT relname FROM pg_class WHERE relrowsecurity = true AND relname LIKE '<table_pattern>'

successCriteria.criterion: All tables are queryable and accessible
successCriteria.validation: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM <each_new_table> executes without error

successCriteria.criterion: No PostgreSQL errors in logs during migration
successCriteria.validation: docker logs ectropy-postgres-1 --tail 100 | grep -i 'ERROR\|FATAL' returns no matches

successCriteria.criterion: Application services restart successfully after migration
successCriteria.validation: docker-compose restart <services> completes successfully, all services healthy

successCriteria.criterion: Existing data remains intact (no data loss)
successCriteria.validation: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM <existing_tables> matches pre-migration counts

supportedQueries.returns: Array of migration phases with ordered steps
List of validation checks to perform before migration
supportedQueries.purpose: Assess migration readiness and identify potential issues

supportedQueries.returns: Detailed backup creation and verification steps
supportedQueries.purpose: Understand backup process and rollback capability
Retrieve complete rollback plan if migration fails

supportedQueries.returns: Commands for dry-run execution without database changes
supportedQueries.purpose: Test migration safety without applying changes
Verify migration success and data integrity

supportedQueries.returns: Database health and performance monitoring queries
supportedQueries.purpose: Monitor migration impact on database performance

supportedQueries.returns: Complete list of success criteria with validation methods
supportedQueries.purpose: Determine if migration meets all requirements

supportedQueries.returns: Risk assessment based on migration complexity
supportedQueries.purpose: Calculate risk level and recommend precautions

decisionSupport: Recommend mandatory dry-run before production migration execution
Suggest backup verification before proceeding with schema changes
Alert if migration has already been applied (idempotent check)
Recommend rollback if post-migration validation fails
Suggest monitoring queries for 24-hour post-migration health checks
Calculate migration risk based on database size, complexity, and operation types
Recommend service restart timing to minimize user impact
Suggest breaking large migrations into smaller incremental steps
Alert to potentially destructive operations (DROP, TRUNCATE) requiring extra caution

knownIssues.title: Migration may fail if prerequisite tables missing
knownIssues.impact: Migration aborts with foreign key constraint errors or table not found
knownIssues.mitigation: Pre-flight checks verify prerequisite tables exist before migration
knownIssues.action: Ensure all previous migrations have been applied in sequence before running new migration

knownIssues.title: Backup creation may fail on low disk space
knownIssues.impact: Migration aborts before applying changes, backup incomplete or missing
knownIssues.mitigation: Pre-flight checks monitor disk space and warn if below threshold
knownIssues.action: Ensure sufficient disk space (500+ MB free) before migration, or use -SkipBackup flag (not recommended for production)

knownIssues.title: Active database connections may interfere with schema changes
knownIssues.impact: Migration may hang or fail if tables are locked by active connections
knownIssues.mitigation: Recommend stopping dependent services before migration for complex schema changes
knownIssues.action: Run migration during maintenance window or stop api-gateway and web-dashboard services temporarily

knownIssues.title: Large migrations may exceed default timeout
knownIssues.impact: Migration appears to fail but may still be executing in PostgreSQL
knownIssues.mitigation: Increase timeout value for complex migrations with many tables/indexes
knownIssues.action: Monitor PostgreSQL logs to verify migration completion if timeout occurs

knownIssues.title: Rollback may lose data inserted after migration
knownIssues.impact: Any data inserted into new tables after migration will be lost on rollback
knownIssues.mitigation: Backup is created before migration, not after
knownIssues.action: Document this behavior clearly to operators - rollback restores to pre-migration state only

required.name: POSTGRES_USER
required.description: PostgreSQL superuser for executing migrations (default: postgres)

required.name: POSTGRES_DB
required.description: Target database name for migration (default: ectropy_dev)

optional.name: SKIP_BACKUP
optional.description: Skip backup creation (not recommended, use only in development)

optional.name: DRY_RUN
optional.description: Show what migration would do without applying changes

optional.name: BACKUP_DIR
optional.description: Custom directory for backup file storage (default: backups/migrations)

usageExamples.scenario: Standard production migration with all safety checks
usageExamples.description: Execute migration with automatic backup, all validations, and post-migration verification
notes: This is the recommended approach for production environments
Full database backup is created automatically before any changes
All pre-flight checks and post-migration validations are performed
Rollback capability available via backup file

usageExamples.scenario: Dry-run to preview migration without changes
usageExamples.description: Validate migration readiness and show what would be executed without applying changes
notes: No database modifications are made
All pre-flight validation checks are still performed
Statement counts and migration impact summary are displayed
Use this to test migration safety before production application
Recommended for all first-time migrations in new environments

usageExamples.description: Execute migration without creating backup (faster, but no rollback capability)
notes: Only use in development environments with non-critical data
No rollback capability without backup
Faster execution but higher risk if migration fails
NOT RECOMMENDED for staging or production

usageExamples.description: Execute migration with detailed progress logging for troubleshooting
notes: Shows detailed progress of each validation and execution step
Useful for troubleshooting migration issues

changelog.author: Claude (Enterprise Integration)
changes: Initial runbook creation from PowerShell migration script
8 pre-flight safety checks covering infrastructure, configuration, and security
Detailed backup creation and verification procedures
SQL syntax validation and dangerous operation detection
Complete rollback procedure with 8 steps and verification criteria
Post-migration validation covering tables, views, columns, indexes, and permissions
MCP integration with 9 queryable operations and decision support
12 success criteria with specific validation queries
Monitoring queries and alert thresholds for post-migration health
5 known issues documented with mitigation strategies
4 usage examples for different scenarios (production, dry-run, development, verbose)
Environment variable documentation for all required and optional settings
Related runbooks cross-referenced for comprehensive deployment guidance
